name: Adversary Name
description: Atmospheric description of the adversary's appearance, behavior, and presence. This sets the tone for how the GM describes the creature during encounters.
motives: Primary goals and tactical approaches - how this adversary behaves in combat and what drives its actions

features.name: Passive Feature
features.description: Always-active ability that defines how this adversary behaves or interacts with the environment. No cost, no activation required.

features.name: Action Feature
features.description: An ability that requires the GM to spend Fear and takes the adversary's action. Format: Spend X Fear to [trigger]. [Mechanical effect]. Include spotlight rules, damage values, conditions applied, or other tactical benefits.

features.name: Reaction Feature
features.description: Triggered ability that responds to specific conditions. Format: When [trigger condition], [effect]. Common reactions gain Fear on successful attacks or apply conditions when hit.

features.name: Limited Use Feature
features.description: Once per scene or limited-use ability. Mark a Stress to [trigger effect]. Include summons, area attacks, or powerful one-time abilities. Specify how many times this can be used (Uses: 1, Uses: 2, etc.).

features.name: Minion Feature
features.description: Minion (X): This adversary is defeated when it takes any amount of damage. For every X damage dealt to a Minion adversary, defeat an additional Minion beyond the first. Minions typically have 1 HP and represent swarms or groups.

lore: Additional background, origin story, or campaign-specific context that helps the GM understand this adversary's role in the world and narrative.
